title: Frangula nelsonii
intro: Frangula nelsonii, also known as Nelson's buckthorn, is a plant species in the Rhamnaceae family, characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and dark green, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including relief from digestive issues, such as constipation, and anti-inflammatory effects. Frangula nelsonii contains bioactive constituents like anthraquinones, which are responsible for its laxative properties, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Frangula nelsonii include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
taxonomy: Frangula nelsonii is a plant species in the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the order Rosales and is native to certain regions. The genus Frangula is classified under the family Rhamnaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as buckthorns. Frangula nelsonii is closely related to other Frangula species, including Frangula alnus. The classification of Frangula nelsonii is not extensively documented in widely available sources.
